title: The U.S. Is Forfeiting the Clean-Energy Race to China - WSJ
description: As President Trump doubles down on fossil fuels, the U.S. and China offer competing visions for the future of energy
bodyText: An empty, 1.3-million-square-foot warehouse north of Denver was supposed to be an emblem of cutting-edge manufacturing. Colorado Gov. Jared Polis heralded a California company’s plan to build a battery factory on-site as a push to “power the future.” But an already weakening growth outlook for electric vehicles deteriorated as President Trump’s tax bill wound its way through Congress. Amprius Technologies didn’t wait for the legislation to pass and pulled out of Colorado earlier this year. Copyright ©2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.
